name: fixture-10-medical-device-sales-director
description: Regional Sales Director at a capital-equipment medical device company applying to a VP Sales role at an early-commercial surgical robotics startup. Strong enterprise-health-system selling; the stretch is going from an established brand's playbook to building one.

resume_data.name: Caroline Byrne
key_skills: Health system IDN + GPO contract negotiation
key_achievements: Grew the Mountain West territory from $42M to $78M in 4 years — exceeded plan 11 of 16 quarters.
Led a 14-rep team to #1 ranking in the national sales force for 2 consecutive years; retained 13 of 14 reps through a compensation redesign.

Closed the largest single-system deal in company history — $11.4M across 5 hospitals with a single IDN contract, including a joint clinical-evidence build that the KOL surgeon published 18 months later.

requirements: Proven success building and leading enterprise capital-equipment sales teams
Experience launching new surgical technology into early-adopter health systems
Deep relationships with academic medical centers and innovation-focused IDNs
Track record of designing the sales playbook — not inheriting one
Partnership with marketing, clinical, and product teams on launch strategy
culture_cues: founder-led — CEO is a former surgeon
clinical rigor is existential — trials and peer-reviewed evidence matter

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the $11.4M IDN deal and the KOL-published clinical evidence — demonstrates both the enterprise selling muscle and the clinical-rigor instinct Axon's founder-CEO will recognize immediately.
body_points: Territory growth ($42M → $78M) and team leadership are the enterprise-sales-leadership proof — 14-rep management already at the scale Axon's VP Sales seat will grow into. Frame the team-retention outcome as evidence of builder-quality leadership.

Largest-deal-in-company-history + KOL co-publication demonstrates the academic medical center + clinical evidence mindset the JD flags; this is the playbook Axon needs built, not just executed. Explicitly reframe current role's playbook-running as muscle that translates to playbook-building.

Value-analysis-committee experience is the enterprise-hospital buying-committee fluency — name this specifically because VAC is a gating stage early-commercial surgical robotics navigates constantly, and it's easy for VP-Sales candidates to underweight in a cover letter.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Acknowledge the step from established-brand to early-commercial directly — running a playbook is different work than building one. Close on the founder-surgeon alignment and the clinical-evidence instinct as the reason to build Axon's playbook rather than stay in the established-brand role.
